R1.justification: §1.1 derives the three structural preconditions (non-interference of redex sites, opaque arguments, no termination dependency) from first principles and §1.2 gives three derived precedents P1-P3 without naming any external result.

R2.justification: §2.2 defines ⇒ inductively; Lemma 2.2.1 is proved by full structural induction (including a P-rule case that handles non-linearity explicitly), Lemma 2.2.2-2.2.3 bridge →/⇒/↠; §2.3 treats Φ well-definedness including the Y-redex subtlety; Triangle Lemma 2.3.1 is proved by induction on ⇒-derivation with all three clauses discharged; §2.4 records non-termination-independence.

R3.justification: All 13 stages are exhibited with a uniform claim/method/gap template; §3.1 provides an explicit 13×13 overlap table with diagonal ε(=) entries; §3.2 gives a per-rule RHS-audit table addressing straddling including the Y-template case.

R4.justification: Theorem 4.1 with a cited proof chain; §4.1 restates counting conventions; §4.2 cleanly separates Q4.2.1 (largest subset = whole baseline, with a structural reason) and Q4.2.2 (minimal non-confluent extension K°: K I x → x with critical pair ⟨I, x⟩ shown non-joinable); §4.3 disambiguates maximal-subset vs maximal-extension.

R5.justification: §5 names both strategy (b) parallel-reduction diamond and strategy (d) executable oracle; /workspaces/task/sim/simulator.py (257 lines) and /workspaces/task/sim/output-final.txt (7 test terms with joinability verdicts) are present and cited; limitations disclosed.

R6.justification: §§6.1-6.6 give six worked examples with explicit step-by-step reduction traces under two orders each, and each closes with a common reduct.

R7.justification: §7.1 addresses strong reduction under binders with parallel-substitution requirements named, §7.2 confluence vs normalization with the WN-uniqueness corollary proved, §7.3 strategy dependence with leftmost/rightmost illustrated on K a (Y f), §7.4 non-orthogonal extensions including an iterative-closure schema (steps 1-4) and multiple worked critical-pair cases.

R8.justification: Gaps are explicitly named: §3.2 (straddling audit), §5.2 (oracle depth/size caps), §7.4.4 (1-rule extension space unenumerated), and a dedicated §8 'Disclosed gaps' list enumerating four items.

R9.justification: The document opens with a 'Counting conventions' block defining term, occurrence, redex, one-step rewrite, overlap, critical pair, parallel step, residual, and confluence; §4.1 restates them at the verdict.

R10.justification: On-disk trace: iterations/attempt-01.md (45466 B), .eval-report-01.json logging 7 gaps, sim/output-run1.txt and output-final.txt. The final differs from attempt-01 (different file sizes and contents) and materially addresses the logged gaps: G1 (substitution lemma tightened with full non-linear case), G2 (§4.2/4.3 explicit separation), G3 (§2.3 Φ well-definedness on Y discussed), G4 (§7.4.3 iterative closure schema + new examples), G5 (13×13 table now present), G7 (RHS audit tabulated).
